summary: Iteration 7 verified end-to-end. Backend 29/29 pytest cases pass against the public URL (Board role + auth/me, Employee scoping with MD/ED Board-only 403 for HR Admin, Review-panel routing for MD/ED board_led + Sarah md_direct_report + Grace finance_ops_report, MD KPIs Board-only with all 8 expected KPIs, Settings-driven leave-types with Compassionate add/remove, Probation 3→6 month change via masters_settings.organisation, PAYE calculator config_source=masters_settings.organisation, CSV exports for employees/pay-bands/budget-allocations with role gating, Form 28 Finance & Operations Manager wording, regression on /access/matrix /settings/masters /budget/envelope). Frontend: Board login reaches /dashboard with role=board, AccessGate renders [data-testid=access-denied-M12] for Employee on /budget with 'Access Restricted — You don't have Read access to module M12' message, HR Admin sees /budget normally, Employees page has [data-testid=export-employees-csv], Compensation Pay-Bands tab has [data-testid=export-pay-bands-csv], Budget page has [data-testid=export-allocations-csv], Sarah Njoroge profile shows role_title='Finance & Operations Manager'. Login page renders 7 quick-demo tiles (HR Admin, Line Manager, Finance, Employee, Solver, MD/Executive, IT Admin) — Board and ED tiles intentionally absent per spec ('Board could be added').

issues: Quick login tiles do not expose data-testid attributes (e.g. data-testid=quick-login-hr-admin) — adding them would harden Playwright coverage. Currently the tiles are matched only by visible text.

action_items: Optional: add Board (and ED) quick-login tiles plus data-testid=quick-login-{role} attributes on each demo tile for testability.
Optional: align /api/leave/types response shape — it currently returns a dict keyed by name with entitlement objects; some clients may expect a list. Document or unify.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/exports.py is concise (102 lines) and role-gates correctly. Note that employees.csv excludes board_only records for non-Board / non-IT-Admin users — consistent with the API list endpoint. Good.

routes/masters_settings.py — write access on the 'organisation' and 'lookups' categories is restricted to IT Admin only (HR Admin gets 403 'You cannot edit ...'). The review-request narrative reads as if HR Admin updates these; if intended, document this gating clearly in the review brief or relax write access on lookups.leave_types specifically. Either way, behaviour is consistent with the previously-iterated access matrix and the IT Admin-only reset policy.

/api/leave/types returns a dict shape (not a list). Considering the review-request wording 'returns leave types pulled from … list', expose a /list variant or convert to list-of-objects to match common consumer expectations. Frontend currently handles dict, so non-blocking.
performance.py md-kpis returns each KPI under key 'kpi' (not 'name' or 'title'). Minor naming inconsistency vs other endpoints — consider 'name' for consistency.
Quick-demo login tiles in Login.js have no data-testid attributes — adding them would let automated tests target tiles directly without text-based selectors.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (7/7 review-request UI checks: Board login & role, AccessGate for Employee on /budget, HR Admin /budget normal, Employees CSV button, Pay Bands CSV button, Allocations CSV button, Sarah role_title in profile)

test_credentials: All 9 accounts from /app/memory/test_credentials.md used; password Solvit@2026. Includes new board (board@solvit.co.ke role=board) and ED (ed@solvit.co.ke). IT Admin (itadmin@solvit.co.ke) is required for masters_settings/{organisation,lookups} mutations and resets.
seed_data_creation: Created two TEST_iter7_probA / TEST_iter7_probB employee rows during the probation-from-settings flow; both deleted in the cleanup test. Demo data was reset once at module start via POST /api/settings/reset-demo-data. masters_settings.organisation and masters_settings.lookups were reset to defaults via /reset endpoints by IT Admin after each mutation. Iteration 8 starts clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression files run sequentially: test_solvit_platform.py → test_iteration_2.py → test_iteration_3.py → test_iteration_4.py → test_iteration_6.py → test_iteration_7.py. Run all: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Iter 7 introduces /api/exports/* (CSV streaming endpoints), /api/performance/md-kpis (Board/Executive/IT Admin only — placed BEFORE /{review_id} in route ordering, verified to work), board role in utils/auth.py ROLES, board_only filter in employees list+detail, ed_id detection in review-panel routing returns panel_type=board_led with note mentioning Executive Director, finance_ops_report panel for Grace/Daniel, settings-driven leave types via lookups.leave_types and probation_period_months via organisation, PAYE/NSSF/SHA all read masters_settings.organisation. AccessGate frontend wraps M10/M11/M12/M13/M14/M15/M18/M19 routes. CSV download buttons use cookie-auth window.location.href; backend Content-Disposition includes filename. Note: organisation/lookups categories require IT Admin role (HR Admin gets 403 — important when scripting setting mutations).
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found in iteration 7. Initial test run (8 failures) was caused entirely by test-script issues, not product issues: (1) calling reset-demo-data inside a fixture re-generated employee IDs after they were already cached on the test class (fixed by moving reset to a session-autouse fixture that runs once at module start before any IDs are captured); (2) MD-KPI test asserted on key 'name'/'title' but the API returns key 'kpi' (fixed); (3) leave-types response is a dict not a list (test now handles both shapes); (4) HR Admin cannot mutate masters_settings.organisation or .lookups by design — IT Admin is required (test now uses it_admin fixture for those mutations and resets). After these test-side adjustments, all 29 backend cases pass and all 7 frontend review-request checks pass.
